corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: split ثَمَّ locative/adverbial from ثُمَّ sequence before rich certification
drill_prompt: Given ثُمَّ and ثَمَّ, identify which one links clauses and which one needs locative/adverbial review.
learner_explanation: The two words look close, but one connects events while the other points to a place or there-ness.
nahw_lesson: ثُمَّ links sequence between clauses; ثَمَّ is locative/adverbial and needs its own attachment.
sarf_lesson: Strict vocalized surface and POS/function must be preserved; norm-like recall cannot join ثَمَّ and ثُمَّ.
what_failed: The locative/adverbial particle appeared in the same inventory as the sequence particle and inherited the same hover.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: split إِذَا temporal-condition from إِذًا inferential/result
drill_prompt: Classify إِذَا and إِذًا in two examples and name the clause relation required for each.
learner_explanation: Both can be rendered with English then in some contexts, but they teach different sentence jobs.
nahw_lesson: إِذَا opens a temporal/conditional frame; إِذًا marks an inferential or result relation.
sarf_lesson: The final alif/tanwīn surface is part of the certifying form.
what_failed: Two entries shared one row family and one gloss despite different clause functions.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: classify ما+ذا as interrogative/relative/etc. in context
drill_prompt: For مَاذَا يُرِيدُ, identify whether ما is interrogative and what clause evidence proves it.
learner_explanation: ماذا can ask a question, but the context must prove that job before the hover teaches it.
nahw_lesson: The function of ما decides the learner hover; a list of possible meanings is not certification.
sarf_lesson: Composite function tokens should preserve their visible pieces without forcing one lemma-family gloss.
what_failed: A blended gloss was treated as a usable fallback instead of a precise function decision.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: split أَنَا independent pronoun from أَنَّا subordinator+pronoun
drill_prompt: Sort أَنَا and أَنَّا into pronoun versus subordinator+pronoun, then name the visible proof.
learner_explanation: أَنَا means I; أَنَّا belongs to a different structure and cannot explain this token.
nahw_lesson: An independent pronoun and a clause-introducing particle have different syntactic jobs.
sarf_lesson: Shadda and pronoun surface are certifying morphology; norm-like equality is recall only.
what_failed: The pronoun entry inherited a subordinating gloss through a shadda/surface collision.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: account for لَيْتَ plus first-person suffix نِي
drill_prompt: Segment لَيْتَنِي and explain why a bare لَيْتَ gloss is incomplete.
learner_explanation: لَيْتَنِي is not just wishing; it says if only I / would that I.
nahw_lesson: لَيْتَ governs a wishing structure; the attached pronoun contributes the speaker/object role.
sarf_lesson: Visible suffix pronouns on particle-like hosts must be preserved in the whole-token hover.
what_failed: The hover explained the host particle while hiding the attached first-person contribution.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: no live repair in this tranche; component candidates may feed rich breakdown only
drill_prompt: Explain why فَـ or هُمْ cannot be used as the whole hover for فَأَهْلَكْنَاهُمْ.
learner_explanation: The word contains several pieces, but none of those pieces alone is the hover for the whole word.
nahw_lesson: The fā function and object suffix can be explained after whole-token syntax is known, but cannot authorize propagation.
sarf_lesson: A composed verb token needs host, proclitic, and suffix analysis; component candidates stay subordinate to the exact token.
what_failed: Component-level فَـ and هُمْ candidates are useful, but they must not certify the whole composed verb token.
